summary: postcss-animatecss ships an obfuscated fetch-and-eval backdoor in the plugin factory
details: index.js embeds a heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io RC4 string-array + control-flow flattening) block inside the exported PostCSS plugin factory. When the plugin is invoked during a PostCSS/Tailwind build, the block decodes a remote URL from string constants, appends Date.now() and option-derived query parameters, fetches JSON via `fetch`, base64-decodes the response `message` field, and passes the decoded bytes to `new Function('require', _)(require)` in a retry loop of up to 10 attempts. This executes attacker-controlled JavaScript with `require` in scope, giving the remote payload full Node privileges on every consumer build machine that uses the plugin. The obfuscation is applied only to the payload block while the surrounding PostCSS wrapper is plain — deliberate concealment, not build-time minification. The `postcss-animatecss` name mimics legitimate PostCSS/animate.css ecosystem plugins, positioning the package to be pulled into Tailwind/PostCSS build configurations.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
